_notes.trend_sleeve_enabled: OFF by default. When true, runs a small spot long-only maker trend-follower (src/trading/trend_sleeve.py) during CONFIRMED up-trends, governed by the same weekly kill switch. Enable only after paper testing.
_notes.trend_sleeve_pct: Fraction of total_capital allocated to the trend sleeve when enabled (0.10 = ~£17). One position at a time.
_notes.spacing_pct: Grid level spacing as % of price. 1.0% base (net ~0.50%/fill above 0.25% maker fee). Min viable: 0.55%. Widens to 1.2-1.6% in confirmed trend/volatile regimes.
_notes.range_pct: Recenter threshold: grid recenters when price moves more than this % from calibration_price. Also used as stale-order distance.
_notes.levels: Total grid orders. 4 keeps order sizes above the exchange minimum at the £170/20%-active size. Regime-aware buy/sell skew.
_notes.capital_pct: Fraction of total_capital to deploy. 0.20 = ~£34 active per week (≥80% reserve). Auto-stepped DOWN by CDaR in drawdown (risk_manager), and to the regime preset on a confirmed trend.
_notes.total_capital: Start £170. Refreshed automatically from live crypto.com portfolio (USDT + BTC value) at each run. Manual value is the fallback.
_notes.gbp_usd_rate: GBP/USD rate for P&L reporting. Update in .env.
_notes.kill_pct: Weekly loss limit as fraction of total_capital (0.10 = 10% = ~£17). Tightened to 0.08 in trending_dn regime.
_notes.minimum_capital: Formula: 0.0001 * BTC_price * levels / capital_pct / gbp_usd_rate. At BTC ~$73k: 4 levels @ 20% -> ~£115 min. Bot enforces this at runtime and alerts if breached.
